intro: Specklinia jalapensis, also known as Jalapa orchid, is a species of orchid native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. Bioactive constituents of Specklinia jalapensis include jalapin, a glycoside responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other iridoid glycosides and flavonoids. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat colds, coughs, and fever.
